Patch Set 5:

> given the client can send <4K I'd suggest to start with a smaller bs.

I'm not sure if this is a good idea because clients usually send page aligned RPC and only the last page in the object could be less than 4K.

Patch Set 5:

this is exactly the point - to support massive writes of tiny files.

in that case, it's probably okay.

Patch Set 7:

(3 comments)

One remaining issue is that there is no test for this patch. Either it should be tested manually against a patched and unpatched server, and the test performance results put in the commit comment. Based on recent test results for random writes on ZFS using explicit recordsize settings, I'd think that the automatic blocksize selection could improve random write performance significantly. 

An automated test written that at least shows the blocksize is working correctly for sequential and random IO would also be good, no need to measure performance.

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

Sure, I will make some test cases for this patch.

parents.subject: LU-8006 ptlrpc: specify ordering of TBF policy rules

commit.subject: LU-4865 zfs: grow block size by write pattern
commit.message:
LU-4865 zfs: grow block size by write pattern

This patch grows the block size by write RPC. The osd-zfs blocksize
used to be fixed at 128KB, which is too big for random write and
too small for seqential write.

This patch decides the block size by the first few RPCs. If the first
few RPCs are sequential, mostly it will pick maximum block size for
the object; otherwise, a feasible block size will be picked by the
RPC size.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I66f7cbdc2b5e0365058b152b4865b00cdabb0cf3
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18441
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Don Brady <don.brady@intel.com>
